questions.prompt: A 3-year-old child rarely responds when their name is called, does not point to share interest with caregivers, and shows little spontaneous sharing of enjoyment with others. Which DSM-5-TR ASD domain does this behaviour primarily reflect?
options: Deficits in social communication and social interaction

questions.prompt: During a developmental assessment, a child repeatedly flaps their hands and scripts lines from a favorite cartoon whenever excited. According to DSM-5-TR ASD criteria, which category does this best represent?

A researcher shows participants animated triangles and circles interacting on a screen. Neurotypical individuals describe the shapes as 'bullying' or 'playing,' while some autistic individuals describe only physical movements. Which cognitive concept is primarily being assessed?

A clinician observes that a child speaks fluently but frequently repeats entire phrases from television shows in conversation rather than generating novel responses. Which ASD communication feature is this?
A child insists on following the exact same morning routine and becomes extremely distressed when a teacher cancels a planned field trip. Which ASD criterion does this behaviour best represent?
A child spends hours memorizing train timetables and becomes intensely focused on this topic to the exclusion of other activities. Which DSM-5-TR ASD feature does this illustrate?
A child becomes extremely distressed by certain clothing textures and frequently covers their ears when hearing loud sounds that others tolerate easily. Which ASD feature is most relevant?
options: Hyper- or hypo-reactivity to sensory input

questions.prompt: A child appears socially typical at school but has emotional meltdowns at home due to exhaustion from imitating peers' social behaviors throughout the day. Which concept best explains this phenomenon?
Research suggests autism is diagnosed approximately four times more often in boys than girls. However, some researchers argue this may partly reflect what issue?
A clinician notes that a child avoids eye contact, struggles with conversational turn-taking, and has difficulty interpreting sarcasm. These challenges primarily fall under which DSM-5-TR category?
Which historical theory incorrectly proposed that autism was caused by emotionally distant parenting?
Why is the 'refrigerator mother' hypothesis now considered harmful and incorrect?
options: It blamed families and delayed biological research

questions.prompt: Which factor has been identified as one of the strongest predictors of long-term outcomes for individuals with ASD?
Early signs of autism often appear between 6 and 18 months. Which of the following is one of the earliest observed indicators?
A parent reports their toddler rarely follows another person’s gaze or pointing gesture toward an object. Which developmental process may be impaired?
Research indicates the apparent rise in autism prevalence is likely influenced by which factor?
Which of the following best describes the DSM-5-TR change compared to DSM-IV diagnostic categories for autism?
options: Separate disorders were merged into a single autism spectrum diagnosis
Autism was removed from neurodevelopmental disorders
Communication deficits were removed from criteria

questions.prompt: An autistic adolescent reports anxiety when transitioning out of school into adult life due to changes in routines and increased independence. Which developmental issue does this reflect?
Which statement best reflects the neurodiversity perspective discussed in the lecture?
options: Autism should always be eliminated medically
Autism is solely a disorder with no strengths
Autism can be both a disability and part of identity

questions.prompt: Which strength is commonly reported among many individuals with ASD?
options: Strong long-term memory for detailed information
